questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, to which class of beings does the tyrant-king Hiranyakashipu belong?
questions.explanation: Hiranyakashipu is a Daitya, a class of asura, in the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: From which deity did Hiranyakashipu obtain his famous protective boon?
questions.explanation: Through severe austerities, Hiranyakashipu won his boon from Brahma.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu finally destroyed the tyrant Hiranyakashipu?
questions.explanation: Narasimha, the man-lion avatar, slew Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: Who was the devout son of Hiranyakashipu whose worship of Vishnu enraged the king?
questions.explanation: Prahlada, son of Hiranyakashipu, was an unwavering devotee of Vishnu.

questions.q: Which deity did Hiranyakashipu forbid his subjects to worship, having sworn vengeance against him?
questions.explanation: Hiranyakashipu hated Vishnu, who had slain his brother, and forbade his worship.

questions.q: Hiranyakashipu's brother, also slain by Vishnu, was which Daitya who dragged the earth into the cosmic ocean?
questions.explanation: Hiranyaksha, the brother of Hiranyakashipu, was slain by Vishnu as the boar Varaha.

questions.q: At what time of day did Narasimha slay Hiranyakashipu, so as to be neither day nor night?
questions.explanation: Narasimha killed him at twilight, which is neither day nor night.

questions.q: From what object did Narasimha burst forth to attack Hiranyakashipu in his own hall?
questions.explanation: Narasimha emerged from a pillar in Hiranyakashipu's palace.

questions.q: What is the form of the avatar Narasimha who killed Hiranyakashipu?
questions.explanation: Narasimha has the form of a man-lion, half-man and half-lion.

questions.q: In which major text is the story of Hiranyakashipu and Prahlada most fully narrated?
questions.explanation: The episode is most fully told in the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: Hiranyakashipu's boon stated he could not be slain by 'man or beast'. How did Narasimha circumvent this clause?
options: By being neither fully man nor fully beast
questions.explanation: As a man-lion, Narasimha was neither wholly man nor wholly beast.

questions.q: Where did Narasimha place Hiranyakashipu to slay him, so that it was 'neither indoors nor outdoors'?
options: In the middle of the ocean
Across his own lap, on the threshold of a doorway
Atop a chariot in the sky
questions.explanation: Narasimha sat on the threshold and laid the king across his lap, neither inside nor outside.

questions.q: With what did Narasimha kill Hiranyakashipu, so as not to use any conventional weapon?
questions.explanation: Narasimha tore the demon apart with his claws rather than a weapon.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, where did Hiranyakashipu perform the austerities that won him his boon?
questions.explanation: Hiranyakashipu performed his great penance on Mount Mandara.

questions.q: While Hiranyakashipu was absent performing penance, which deva's army troubled his kingdom and seized his queen?
questions.explanation: Indra, king of the devas, attacked and carried off Hiranyakashipu's pregnant queen Kayadhu.

questions.q: Which sage sheltered Hiranyakashipu's pregnant queen and taught devotion to the unborn Prahlada?
questions.explanation: The sage Narada protected the queen and instructed Prahlada in the womb.

questions.q: By what means did Hiranyakashipu first attempt to destroy his son Prahlada, according to the Bhagavata Purana?
options: A single duel with a sword
questions.explanation: The Purana describes attempts on Prahlada by poison, serpents, fire, and more, all of which failed.

questions.q: Which demoness, Hiranyakashipu's sister, tried to burn Prahlada in fire but was herself consumed?
questions.explanation: Holika, immune to fire by a boon, sat with Prahlada in flames but burned instead, while he was spared.

questions.q: Whose grandson is the generous asura king Bali, descended from Hiranyakashipu's line through Prahlada?
questions.explanation: Bali was the grandson of Prahlada, the son of Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: In Hiranyakashipu's boon from Brahma, what did the clause 'neither by day nor by night' protect him from?
options: Loss of his power of flight
Death at any hour of day or night
Loss of his command over time
questions.explanation: The boon shielded Hiranyakashipu from death at any time, a clause Narasimha satisfied by striking at twilight, which is neither day nor night.

questions.q: When the boy Prahlada was asked where his Lord Vishnu was, what was his celebrated reply?
questions.explanation: Prahlada affirmed that Vishnu is omnipresent, present everywhere, even in the pillar.

questions.q: The name 'Hiranyakashipu' is often understood to mean which of the following?
questions.explanation: 'Hiranya-kashipu' is read as 'golden garment' or 'clad in gold', reflecting his love of wealth.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, who was the father of both Hiranyakashipu and Hiranyaksha?
questions.explanation: Hiranyakashipu and Hiranyaksha were sons of the sage Kashyapa and Diti.

questions.q: Who was the mother of Hiranyakashipu, a daughter of Daksha and wife of Kashyapa?
questions.explanation: Diti, mother of the Daityas, bore Hiranyakashipu and Hiranyaksha to Kashyapa.

questions.q: In one Puranic tradition, Hiranyakashipu and Hiranyaksha are said to be a cursed incarnation of which gatekeepers of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The doorkeepers Jaya and Vijaya, cursed by the Kumaras, are said to be born as Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: What was the name of Hiranyakashipu's chief queen and the mother of Prahlada?
questions.explanation: Kayadhu was Hiranyakashipu's queen and the mother of Prahlada.

questions.q: Besides Prahlada, the Bhagavata Purana names other sons of Hiranyakashipu. Which of these was among them?
questions.explanation: The Purana lists sons such as Samhlada, Anuhlada, and Hrada alongside Prahlada; Indrajit was Ravana's son, Banasura was Bali's son, and Virochana was Prahlada's son.

questions.q: Hiranyakashipu's tutors, who tried to wean Prahlada from devotion to Vishnu, were the sons of which preceptor of the asuras?
questions.explanation: Shanda and Amarka, sons of the asura guru Shukracharya, were Prahlada's teachers.

questions.q: After Narasimha's fierce rage at the death of Hiranyakashipu, who in the Bhagavata Purana finally calmed the avatar with prayer?
questions.explanation: It was the devotee Prahlada whose prayers pacified the wrathful Narasimha.

questions.q: The festival of Holi is popularly connected to the Hiranyakashipu legend through which event?
options: The burning of Holika and the saving of Prahlada
questions.explanation: Holi is associated with the burning of Holika while the devotee Prahlada was preserved.
